heading.purpose: Page titles, card/dialog titles, field legends — anything carrying a *-title data-slot.

shadows.name: shadow-clay
shadows.value: inset 0 1px 0 color-mix(in oklch, white, transparent 60%), 0 1px 1px color-mix(in oklch, var(--foreground), transparent 92%), 0 4px 10px -4px color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary), transparent 82%), 0 18px 36px -16px color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary), transparent 68%)
shadows.purpose: Earned glow for the single most important surface on a screen.

shadows.name: shadow-clay-warm
shadows.value: inset 0 1px 0 color-mix(in oklch, white, transparent 60%), 0 1px 1px color-mix(in oklch, var(--foreground), transparent 92%), 0 4px 10px -4px color-mix(in oklch, var(--accent-warm), transparent 78%), 0 18px 36px -16px color-mix(in oklch, var(--accent-warm), transparent 62%)
shadows.purpose: Celebratory variant, paired with the warm accent only.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Default action button across the app.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #1D4ED8; color: #F4F8FC; padding: 0.5rem 0.625rem; border-radius: 0.5625rem; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.875rem; border: none; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), transform 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: color-mix(in oklch, #1D4ED8, transparent 20%); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(37,99,235,0.5); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Warm CTA Button
components.description: The single celebratory/high-emphasis CTA per screen, using the one warm accent in the system.
components.css: .ds-btn-warm { background: #C2410C; color: #FFF7ED; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; border-radius: 0.5625rem; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.875rem; border: none; box-shadow: 0 4px 10px -4px rgba(194,65,12,0.35), 0 18px 36px -16px rgba(194,65,12,0.3); transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-warm:hover { background: color-mix(in oklch, #C2410C, transparent 15%); } .ds-btn-warm:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(194,65,12,0.4); }

components.name: Weak Point Card
components.description: Signature component: surfaces one recurring mistake with its source evidence.
components.html:
Subject-verb agreement
Seen 4× · last in "Morning routine" recording, 2 days ago
components.css: .ds-card { background: #FFFFFF; border-radius: 1.15rem; box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.4); box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px rgba(16,27,45,0.1); padding: 1rem; } .ds-card-title { font-family: 'Fredoka', sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 1rem; color: #101B2D; letter-spacing: -0.01em; } .ds-card-meta { font-family: 'Nunito Variable', sans-serif; font-size: 0.8rem; color: #56708A; margin-top: 0.25rem; }

components.name: Text Input
components.css: .ds-input { height: 2rem; width: 100%; border-radius: 0.5625rem; border: 1px solid #D3E3F3; background: transparent; padding: 0.25rem 0.625rem; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #101B2D; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #56708A; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: #2563EB; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(37,99,235,0.5); }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: Active/hover nav item inside the dark Deep Study Navy sidebar.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; border-radius: 0.75rem; font-family: 'Nunito Variable', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #DCE8FB; text-decoration: none; transition: background 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.12); } .ds-nav-item-active { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.12); color: #FFFFFF; }

narrative.overview: RemeLearning is a calm-blue study surface that runs a warm, energetic pulse underneath it — "Focused Blue" (the palette's own name in the codebase) carries the credibility and concentration a learner needs to trust what the app tells them about their own mistakes, while the orange accent, motion, and copy carry the encouragement. The two are deliberately split: the base palette earns trust, the accent and motion earn momentum. This system explicitly rejects the generic AI-SaaS dashboard look — no hero-metric tiles, no gradient text, no side-stripe accent borders, no identical icon+heading+text card grids repeated down the page — and it rejects childish gamification (mascots, confetti, badge walls). Energy here reads as responsive and warm, not loud. Every weak point or recommendation the app surfaces is evidence pulled from the learner's own recordings; the interface should always make that traceability visible (which recording, how often, how recently) rather than presenting a recommendation as generic advice.
keyCharacteristics: Calm blue base (background, primary, sidebar) for trust and low cognitive load
One warm orange accent (accent-warm) reserved for CTAs and celebratory/motivating moments
Soft, generous corner radii (1.15rem base) — a rounded, approachable geometry
Flat-plus-glow: nearly flat surfaces, with a soft colored glow (shadow-clay) reserved for elevated, primary elements
Two-weight type system: Fredoka for headings, Nunito for body

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Ember Orange (accent-warm) appears on at most one element per screen — the single primary CTA or a celebratory state.

rules.name: The No-Gray-Text Rule
rules.body: Body and label text never drop to a flat neutral gray; muted-foreground is always a tint of the primary hue, kept at >=4.5:1 contrast.

rules.name: The Two-Voice Rule
rules.body: Only two families exist in this system: Fredoka for headings/titles, Nunito for everything else.

rules.name: The Earned Glow Rule
rules.body: A card gets shadow-clay only if it's the single most important surface on its screen; everything else stays flat with a hairline ring.

dos: Do keep Ember Orange (#C2410C) to one element per screen.
Do tint every neutral toward the primary blue hue rather than reaching for flat gray.
Do reserve shadow-clay for the single most important surface per screen.
Do ground every weak-point/recommendation card in traceable evidence.
Do carry energy through motion, copy, and the warm accent — not by widening the base palette's saturation.
donts: Don't use hero-metric-card templates, gradient text, or side-stripe colored borders.
Don't repeat the identical icon+heading+text card grid across sections.
Don't add tiny uppercase eyebrows above every section.
Don't introduce mascots, confetti, or badge-wall gamification.
Don't drop body or label text to flat gray.
Don't add a third font family for emphasis.
